summary: Carbon Labs is IBM's community-driven incubation tier: experimental, independently-published @carbon-labs/* packages built ON TOP of core Carbon (@carbon/react, @carbon/web-components, @carbon/grid). It is where things prototype BEFORE they might land in @carbon/react or @carbon/ibm-products.
relationToCore: Stability ladder: @carbon/react & @carbon/web-components (stable) -> @carbon/ibm-products (productized patterns) -> @carbon-labs/* (experimental incubation). Labs reuses core Carbon's tokens/themes/grid. DEPENDENCY MODEL (verified against the npm registry): labs React packages do NOT pin @carbon/react directly — they pull shared Carbon code via @carbon-labs/utilities / @carbon-labs/primitives and declare react / react-dom (or @carbon/ibm-products) as PEER deps, so you install Carbon in your host app. Web-component packages mostly depend on @carbon/web-components (a few depend on lit directly). Reach for labs only when core Carbon and ibm-products lack the component you need.
stabilityWarning: EVERY @carbon-labs package is 0.x and a minor version is cut on EVERY PR merge to main (hence high numbers like react-ui-shell@0.103.0). Treat all of it as unstable: PIN the exact version, expect breaking changes between minors, and confirm the live API at the linked Storybook before writing code. The versions in this catalog are a 2026-06 snapshot and will be stale.
apiCaveat: This is a curated, npm-registry-verified catalog (name / framework / status / install / dependsOn / gap-filled), NOT a scraped prop reference. Exact named exports, custom-element tag names, and props churn fast in labs — verify them at the package's Storybook (docsBase) rather than trusting a remembered identifier.

disambiguation.note: TWO different Carbon chat libraries exist (VERIFIED: distinct npm scopes AND distinct repos). (1) @carbon/ai-chat — the PRODUCTIZED, framework-flexible conversational AI UI (React <ChatContainer> / web components), repo github.com/carbon-design-system/carbon-ai-chat; this is what the carbon_ai_chat MCP tool covers. (2) @carbon-labs/ai-chat — the EXPERIMENTAL labs Lit web component (lit + custom-elements.json) with built-in chart/data-viz answers (rendered via vega/vega-lite), repo github.com/carbon-design-system/carbon-labs. They are NOT the same package. For production, prefer @carbon/ai-chat (use carbon_ai_chat). Use @carbon-labs/ai-chat only for prototyping or when you specifically want the labs chart-answer chat.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/ai-chat
packages.purpose: Experimental conversational AI chat UI as a Lit web component, with built-in support for rendering data-visualization answers (charts via vega/vega-lite), mermaid diagrams, math, and code highlighting inside messages.
packages.components: chat container, message list/bubbles, prompt input, and chart/diagram/code answer rendering (clabs-chat-* custom elements)
packages.gap: A drop-in Carbon-styled chat surface that natively renders chart (vega), diagram, and code answers, for prototyping AI assistants without building the message UI yourself.
packages.note: NOT the same as @carbon/ai-chat (see disambiguation). For production conversational UIs prefer @carbon/ai-chat / the carbon_ai_chat tool. Charts are rendered with vega/vega-lite, NOT @carbon/charts.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/wc-ai-tag
packages.purpose: The 'Carbon for AI' AI-label tag — the small badge that marks AI-generated or AI-assisted content, as a web component.
packages.components: AI label / AI tag custom element
packages.gap: A reusable AI-label/AI-tag primitive for 'Carbon for AI' compliance when marking AI content. (Core @carbon/react also has an AILabel/slug; this is the labs web-component flavor.)
packages.note: An earlier @carbon-labs/ai-tag (verified to exist, latest 0.8.0) also exists; wc-ai-tag is the current web-component package.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/react-ui-shell
packages.purpose: Extended application UI Shell (header + side navigation) building on core Carbon's UIShell — the most mature React labs package.
packages.components: app shell, header, side nav (extended beyond @carbon/react UIShell)
packages.gap: A richer, more opinionated app-shell than core @carbon/react UIShell when you need more shell behavior out of the box.
packages.note: Does not pin @carbon/react itself — expects Carbon in the host app.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/react-animated-header
packages.purpose: Animated / collapsing page header that transitions as the user scrolls.
packages.gap: A polished scroll-collapsing header interaction that core Carbon does not ship.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/react-resizer
packages.purpose: Resize bar / resizable panel layout for splitting a view into adjustable regions.
packages.components: resizer / resize handle for split layouts
packages.gap: Adjustable split/resizable panels — and the SUPPORTED replacement for the deprecated react-split-panel.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/react-split-panel
packages.purpose: Split panel layout (DEPRECATED).
packages.note: DEPRECATED — do not use for new work; use @carbon-labs/react-resizer instead. Note: deprecated by the package DESCRIPTION only (not via npm's `deprecated` flag), so `npm install` will NOT print a warning.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/react-theme-settings
packages.purpose: Theme switcher / theme-settings panel for letting users pick a Carbon theme (white/g10/g90/g100) at runtime.
packages.components: theme settings panel / theme switcher UI
packages.gap: A ready-made UI for runtime theme switching (core Carbon ships <Theme>/<GlobalTheme> but not a settings panel).

packages.name: @carbon-labs/react-whats-new
packages.purpose: 'What's new' announcement / release-highlights UI for surfacing product updates to users.
packages.gap: A pre-built product-announcement / changelog-highlights surface.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/react-processing
packages.purpose: Processing / progress indicator for long-running operations.
packages.gap: A processing-state indicator variant beyond core Loading/ProgressBar.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/react-text-highlighter
packages.purpose: Highlight / annotate spans of text (e.g. search-term or AI-citation highlighting).
packages.gap: Inline text highlighting (search matches, citations, annotations) — not in core Carbon.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/react-tag-input
packages.purpose: Tag input field — type-to-add chips/tags within an input.
packages.gap: A multi-value tag/chip entry field (core Carbon has Tag, but not a tag-entry input).

packages.name: @carbon-labs/react-first-time-orientation
packages.purpose: First-time user onboarding / orientation flow (intro coachmarks/tour).
packages.gap: A guided first-run onboarding/orientation experience.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/react-calendar
packages.purpose: Calendar component for date display/selection.
packages.gap: A full calendar view (core Carbon DatePicker is an input/flatpickr, not a standalone calendar surface).

packages.name: @carbon-labs/react-date-picker
packages.purpose: Date picker for React (labs variant, related to the labs Calendar).
packages.gap: An alternative date picker exploration alongside core Carbon DatePicker.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/react-style-picker
packages.purpose: Style / appearance picker for choosing visual styles (e.g. theme or style presets).
packages.components: style / appearance picker (React wrapper around the wc-style-picker web component via @lit/react)
packages.gap: A style-selection UI for user personalization.
packages.note: Verified to be a React-over-web-component wrapper (wraps @carbon-labs/wc-style-picker via @lit/react), not a hand-written React component.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/react-plane-stack-3d
packages.purpose: 3D plane-stack visualization (layered planes rendered with React Three Fiber).
packages.gap: A 3D layered-plane data/visual exploration — well outside core Carbon's 2D component scope.
packages.note: three.js / React Three Fiber are PEER dependencies (you install them in the host app).

packages.name: @carbon-labs/wc-global-header
packages.purpose: Application global header as a web component — the most mature labs WC package.
packages.gap: A ready-made app header for non-React / web-component stacks.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/wc-style-picker
packages.purpose: Style / appearance picker as a web component.
packages.gap: Style-selection UI for web-component apps.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/wc-empty-state
packages.purpose: Empty-state pattern (illustration + message + action) as a web component.
packages.gap: A reusable empty-state component (Carbon documents empty states as a PATTERN but ships no component for it).

packages.name: @carbon-labs/wc-date-picker
packages.purpose: Date picker as a web component.
packages.gap: Date picker exploration for web-component apps.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/wc-resizer
packages.purpose: Resizable panel layout as a web component.
packages.gap: Adjustable split/resizable panels for web-component apps.
packages.note: Verified: depends on `lit` directly (NOT @carbon/web-components). React flavor: @carbon-labs/react-resizer.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/network-graph
packages.purpose: Force-directed network graph visualization as a web component.
packages.gap: A force-directed node-link graph — a real data-viz gap that neither @carbon/charts nor core Carbon fills.
packages.note: Verified deps include force-graph (^1.43.x) + @carbon/web-components + @carbon/grid. Complements @carbon/charts (carbon_chart tool) for relationship/topology data.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/mdx-components
packages.purpose: MDX doc components used to author Storybook/MDX documentation for labs.
packages.gap: Doc-authoring tooling — not an app UI component.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/utilities
packages.purpose: Shared helper utilities consumed by most labs components (the package through which labs React components pull in Carbon).
packages.gap: Internal shared infrastructure — usually pulled in transitively, not used directly.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/primitives
packages.purpose: Framework-agnostic primitives shared across labs components.
packages.gap: Internal shared infrastructure.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/vscode-snippets
packages.purpose: VSCode snippets for Carbon Design System SCSS / theming.
packages.gap: Editor tooling — not an app UI component.
packages.note: Also distributed as a VSCode extension.

packages.name: @carbon-labs/react-example-button
packages.purpose: Scaffolding/template component demonstrating the labs package structure (labs.status: draft).
packages.note: Template only — NOT for production (package metadata flags labs.status: draft). New labs components are scaffolded via `npx @carbon-labs/create@latest <name>`.
